verifyCommand: uv run --extra vision pytest && uvx ruff check .
acceptanceCriteria: Verification passes (uv run --extra vision pytest && uvx ruff check .)
All non-skipped features completed (passes: true)
All pre-existing tests still pass unchanged
notes: H6 from NEXT_STEPS 'Hardening / rigor backlog': add ONE genuinely independent detector (a duplicate-address ring / link-analysis signal) so 'corroboration' across detectors stops being two views of the same dollars-per-job ratio. This is pure CODE + TDD on SEEDED tmp_path warehouses. CRITICAL GUARDRAILS: (1) NEVER run the `relief-probe` CLI or open data/relief_probe.duckdb — that is the real ~11.3M-loan warehouse; all tests build their own warehouse via warehouse.connect(tmp_path/...). (2) Do NOT hand-write or invent any benchmark/lift NUMBERS into README/docs — real numbers are generated by a human on the full dataset AFTER the loop. Qualitative honesty text only. (3) Entity key is loan_number (str). (4) Reuse existing modules/patterns: detectors/base.py (Detector/Signal), detectors/_cohort.py + stats.py (robust_z, with min_mad floor), detectors/registry.py. Match codebase style: typed, documented, ruff line-length 90. (5) Commit ONE feature per iteration; set its passes=true; update plans/progress.md; re-read plans/guardrails.md every iteration. Detectors must be read-only (never write to the warehouse in run()).

features.title: Address normalization helper (canonical building-level key)
acceptanceCriteria: New module src/relief_probe/detectors/_address.py with normalize_address(address, city, state, zip_code) -> str | None

Returns a canonical UPPER-cased key: collapse internal whitespace, strip punctuation, normalize common USPS street suffixes (STREET->ST, AVENUE->AVE, ROAD->RD, BOULEVARD->BLVD, DRIVE->DR, LANE->LN, COURT->CT, PLACE->PL), and strip unit/suite designators (SUITE/STE/UNIT/APT/'#' + number) so loans in the same building collapse to one key (the ring signal); document the false-positive mode (shared office buildings / registered-agent addresses)
Returns None when the address is missing/blank or too sparse to key reliably (e.g. empty street) so such loans are excluded from ring grouping rather than mis-grouped

tests/test_address.py: equivalence classes map to the SAME key ('123 Main Street'/'123 MAIN ST'/'123 Main St., Suite 200' all equal); distinct buildings map to DIFFERENT keys; blank/None inputs return None; the function is pure (no warehouse access)
Verification passes
features.notes: Pure string function, its own tested module. Unit-stripping is a deliberate design choice (building-level grouping) — document it. No external deps; plain Python/re.

features.title: duplicate_address_ring detector (link-analysis signal)
acceptanceCriteria: New module src/relief_probe/detectors/duplicate_address_ring.py defining DuplicateAddressRingDetector(Detector) with detector_id='duplicate_address_ring', a one-line summary, and __init__(min_ring_size=3, ...)

run(con) reads loans (loan_number, borrower_name, borrower_address, borrower_city, borrower_state, borrower_zip, current_approval_amount), keys each loan via normalize_address, and forms rings = address keys with >= min_ring_size DISTINCT borrower_names (distinct BORROWERS, so one borrower with many loans is NOT a ring); loans whose address key is None are skipped
Each loan in a ring emits a Signal scored monotonically in ring size and total ring dollars (e.g. log1p-scaled), so bigger/denser rings rank higher; score is comparable WITHIN this detector
evidence dict includes: normalized_address, ring_size (distinct borrowers), n_loans, total_ring_amount, and a capped sample of borrower_names
Detector is read-only (never writes to the warehouse)

tests/test_detectors.py (or a new tests/test_ring_detector.py) on a SEEDED tmp_path warehouse: a planted ring of >= min_ring_size distinct borrowers at one (variously-formatted) address all fire; an address with a single borrower holding multiple loans does NOT fire; a ring one below min_ring_size does NOT fire; evidence ring_size counts DISTINCT borrowers
Verification passes
features.notes: Genuinely independent of the $/job detectors — this is a graph/co-location signal. Reuse Signal/base.py. Keep grouping in pandas or SQL; either is fine. Document FP modes (legit shared buildings, registered-agent services, strip malls).

features.title: Register + integrate the ring detector
acceptanceCriteria: registry.py all_detectors() includes DuplicateAddressRingDetector(); update its module docstring 'Live' list
No change needed to runner/scoring/benchmark (they iterate all_detectors generically) — confirm via test, do not special-case

tests: run_all(con) on a seeded warehouse returns a count keyed by 'duplicate_address_ring'; composite_ranking can now yield n_signals up to 3 and a loan flagged by the ring detector AND a $/job detector shows corroboration (n_signals >= 2) with both detector_ids listed
Verification passes
features.notes: Integration only. The composite already does max(percentile) + corroboration generically, so a 3rd detector just works. Prove it with a seeded test that plants a loan tripping ring + payroll.

features.title: Independence validation (orthogonal to the $/job signal)
acceptanceCriteria: A test (tests/test_ring_detector.py) plants a ring whose loans have PERFECTLY NORMAL dollars-per-job (sized like their NAICSxstate peers and below the payroll cap) and asserts: duplicate_address_ring flags them, while naics_cohort_outlier and payroll_cap_exceedance do NOT — demonstrating the ring detector contributes signal the $/job detectors cannot see

Optional small helper (e.g. in detectors/_address.py or a benchmark util) computing the overlap (Jaccard or intersection count) between two detectors' flagged loan sets, with a unit test — used to argue corroboration is across INDEPENDENT views; keep minimal and tested if added
No real-data run and no invented numbers (the real-warehouse independence/lift check is a documented MANUAL post-loop step)
Verification passes
features.notes: This is the POINT of H6 — prove orthogonality on synthetic data. Real-data lift + overlap numbers are generated manually after the loop, not here.

features.title: Docs: detector catalog + NEXT_STEPS (qualitative only)
acceptanceCriteria: README detector catalog gains a duplicate_address_ring entry describing it as the independent link-analysis signal that answers the 'corroboration is two views of the same $/job ratio' critique; state its false-positive modes honestly; NO benchmark/lift numbers

NEXT_STEPS.md: mark H6 done (code + synthetic independence test); add a short 'MANUAL post-loop' note to run `relief-probe score` + `benchmark` on the real warehouse and record real independence/overlap + lift numbers
RESPONSIBLE_USE.md (or README): one line that shared-address rings name real people/addresses and are review leads, not proof of fraud
No fabricated metrics anywhere; qualitative text only
Verification passes
features.notes: Docs only. Honest framing, zero numbers. The human regenerates numbers on real data post-loop.
